metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC to f93a (or a dedicated child) to run the pre-change gate over the eval corpus and persist findings/run, nit-rate, and FP/verify-drop rate as the baseline before any other child merges.
findings.finding: No child explicitly owns recording the pre-change baseline metrics (findings/run, nit-rate, FP/verify-drop rate over the eval corpus) that the parent success criterion requires to make 'measurable improvement' checkable. Child f93a's AC5 records the nit-framing experiment as an eval case but does not assign someone to run the full gate over the corpus pre-change and persist those three numbers.
findings.location: child f93a-b947-558b-469c (closest owner); gap spans entire sibling roster
findings.suggested_fix: Add to f93a's scope: 'Before any prompt/detector child merges, execute the current gate over the eval corpus and record findings_per_run, nit_rate, and verify_drop_rate in a committed baseline artifact (e.g., evals/baseline_metrics.json).' Add a matching AC: '[ ] Pre-change baseline artifact committed with findings/run, nit-rate, FP/verify-drop rate; subsequent children's eval gates compare against it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the code-review eval_solver arm: specify the case schema fields (diff_text / changed_files / ticket context), the prompt_id dispatch string(s), and the named gating scorers with their pass conditions (e.g., recall ≥ X over seeded defect cases, false-accept ≤ Y).
findings.finding: The plan's first AC — 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest' — references an eval_solver code-review arm, but eval_solver.run_case (tool-verified) only handles three prompt_ids ('completion-verifier', 'ticket-quality', 'spec-alignment') plus the plan-review criterion path. No code-review gate arm exists, and the plan never defines the case schema (what fields carry diff_text, changed_files, ticket_id), the prompt_id string(s) to dispatch on, or the output shape the gating scorers will consume.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a story/task to capture the governing principle (Pass-2 genericity, overlay specialization contract) and the content-triggered overlay pattern in a durable artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design doc) — not just a session-log comment.
findings.finding: The plan introduces at least three novel architectural decisions — the governing principle about Pass-2 genericity vs. overlay specialization, the content-triggered overlay pattern (OVERLAY_IDS registry), and the FP-ledger convention — but commits to no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc artifact to capture them. The governing principle is described only as 'locked with user' in the Context narrative, which is not a durable, discoverable artifact.
findings.location: Context (governing principle); Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped' and 'FP-ledger convention … landed'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit AC: 'The governing principle and content-triggered overlay pattern are documented in CLAUDE.md or an ADR committed alongside the OVERLAY_IDS change.' This makes the documentation a gate condition, not an afterthought.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'story A' explicitly in the plan — name it, scope it, and reference it in a decomposition or story list so the baseline-recording sequencing dependency is traceable.
findings.finding: 'story A' is referenced in Success Criteria as a defined, sequenced entity ('story A records the current (pre-change) gate's findings/run…as the baseline') but is never defined, named, or placed in a decomposition anywhere in the plan body, making the measurable-improvement criterion unverifiable and the sequencing dependency invisible.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'story A records the current (pre-change) gate's findings/run…'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Story decomposition' section (even a brief one) that names Story A as 'Baseline recording + eval foundation' and lists it as a prerequisite for all other stories. Then update the Success Criteria to reference 'Story A (Baseline recording)' by its defined name.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate one child as the 'last to merge' owner of criteria_routing.json and add a post-merge validation test (or schema check) that confirms all expected routing entries are present after all three children land.
findings.finding: Three children (da25, f983, 3c5d) all edit criteria_routing.json with 'SHARED — coordinate' notes, but no child owns a merge/sequencing strategy or a test that the combined file is valid after all three edits land. A naive three-way parallel development could silently lose one child's routing entry.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to f983 (as it already declares file_impact): 'After da25 and 3c5d merge, validate criteria_routing.json contains entries for conflict-marker, deletion-impact, and scope-intent. Add a schema/completeness test to eval or CI that fails if any expected routing key is absent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define concrete numeric thresholds for the gating scorers (e.g., recall ≥ X%, FP-drop ≥ Y%, or at minimum 'all seeded recall cases pass and no previously-passing no-fire case regresses') so the eval gate is deterministically evaluable in-session.
findings.finding: Success Criteria state 'higher valid-finding recall and lower advisory nit/FP noise vs those recorded numbers' but no numeric threshold or statistical significance bar is defined, making the gating scorers' pass/fail threshold ambiguous for in-session evaluation.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'success = higher valid-finding recall and lower advisory nit/FP noise vs those recorded numbers'
findings.suggested_fix: At minimum, define the gate as: 'all seeded recall cases fire AND all seeded no-fire cases do not fire'; leave quantitative improvement thresholds to be filled in once the baseline is recorded (Story A).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'prefer AST-LSP' capability assertion: confirm which AST/LSP tools will be available to the verifier agent (native pai_tools addition, a specific MCP server bundled or required in CI, or a best-effort hint to use search_files as a text-only fallback), and document the fallback behavior when AST/LSP tooling is absent.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded … prefer AST-LSP' — implying the verifier will prefer AST or LSP tool calls over text search. However, the rebar agent toolset (pai_tools) provides only read_file/list_directory/search_files/show_ticket and optionally user-configured MCP servers. No AST or LSP tools exist natively; MCP availability is not guaranteed in CI or production deployments.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP).'
findings.suggested_fix: If AST/LSP tooling is not being added to the agent's native tool surface in this epic, change the AC to 'prefer code-grounded search (path:line citations; use search_files for caller/usage lookup)' to match actual tool availability and defer AST/LSP to a follow-on.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before building the eval_solver code-review arm, document why extending eval_solver is preferred over using Inspect AI's native dataset evaluation or promptfoo's diff-input support for this arm, and confirm the diff-fixture materialization strategy.
findings.finding: The plan mandates 'an eval_solver code-review arm' without considering whether Inspect AI (already a declared dependency for evals) or promptfoo already provide a suitable diff-fixture evaluation harness. The existing eval_solver is architected around ticket/plan-text fixtures; a code-review arm would need diff materialization, which is a distinct fixture shape not addressed in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'both halves' of the behavioral-testing standard explicitly in the AC (e.g., 'positive-path assertion half' and 'negative/edge-case assertion half') so the criterion is self-contained and checkable.
findings.finding: 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)' — 'both halves' is undefined in the plan; an executor cannot determine what constitutes completion without external context.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'both halves' with the two named components, e.g.: 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard: (1) assertion that tests cover the stated behavior, and (2) assertion that failure paths / edge cases are exercised.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'enriched' with a specific observable outcome per overlay (e.g., 'each overlay carries the dso-sourced FP-guard items and at least one recall-case in the eval corpus that was previously missed').
findings.finding: 'All five specialist overlays (security, performance, api-compat, db-migrations, llm-prompts) enriched' uses 'enriched' — an effort/subjective term with no observable threshold, making the criterion not evaluable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'All five specialist overlays … enriched'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Inline the exact linter-deference sentence (or a stable reference to where it is canonically recorded) into the AC so the criterion is self-contained.
findings.finding: 'Base prompt carries the experiment-backed positive linter-deference line only' — the plan does not identify which experiment, what the line is, or where it is documented, leaving an executor unable to implement without external research.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Base prompt carries the experiment-backed positive linter-deference line only'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace '#6' with either a full description of the cross-patchset memory feature or an explicit tracker URL/issue title so the scope-exclusion AC is self-contained and verifiable.
findings.finding: '#6' in the final AC bullet ('Cross-patchset memory (#6) split to its own linked epic') is an undefined reference — no item numbered 6 exists anywhere in the plan, making this criterion's verify step ungrounded.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet: 'Cross-patchset memory (#6) split to its own linked epic, not implemented here.'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the bullet to: 'Cross-patchset memory (the capability to carry findings across successive patchsets of the same PR) is split to its own linked epic [link or title], not implemented here.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback / back-out plan for prompt and detector behavioral changes (e.g., versioned prompt snapshots, a feature-flag or staged-rollout switch, or an explicit 'revert to snapshot X' procedure) so that production regressions can be reversed without ad-hoc git archaeology.
findings.finding: The plan changes existing behavior (base prompt, Pass-2 verifier prompt, all five specialist overlays, new conflict-marker detector) but includes no explicit back-out or expand-contract mechanism if the calibrated prompts cause unexpected verdict regressions in production.
findings.location: Success Criteria / Acceptance Criteria (AC lines for base prompt, Pass-2 upgrade, specialist overlays, conflict-marker detector)
findings.suggested_fix: Before each story that ships a prompt change, snapshot the current prompt file(s) under a versioned path (e.g., prompts/v{N}/). Define a one-step rollback procedure (e.g., `make rollback-prompts VERSION=N`) in the epic. For the conflict-marker detector, its fail-open/advisory nature already mitigates rollback risk — document that explicitly as the justification for why no further rollback mechanism is needed for that specific change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval_solver code-review arm design: state whether it extends `run_case` (new prompt_id branch) or creates a parallel runner, how fixture diffs are constructed (e.g., using the `diff_text=` offline seam with a seed diff string), and how `produce_code_review_verdict` (or the gate-dispatch equivalent) is invoked — so implementers don't have to design the seam from scratch.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 requires an 'eval_solver code-review arm' but the existing eval_solver.run_case seam is built for inline-text plan-review finders and store-seeded agentic ops — it has no mechanism for the code-review gate's diff-based workflow. The code-review gate takes a (base, head) git range or a diff_text= input and runs assemble_diff → base → overlays → merge → verify → decide → coach; case_store creates a repo with a single fixture commit but no real diffs. The plan doesn't specify whether the new arm extends run_case (adding a branch for a 'code-review' prompt_id) or creates a parallel scaffolding, how fixture diffs are constructed (the diff_text= offline seam exists but isn't mentioned), or how the gate's produce_code_review_verdict is invoked from the solver. Without this, implementers must design the seam from scratch.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'The code-review eval solver arm accepts a `diff_text` fixture (leveraging the gate's existing `diff_text=` offline seam from `assemble_diff_context`) so no real git history is required; the solver runs `produce_code_review_verdict(diff_text=case["diff_text"], changed_files=case.get("changed_files"), runner=runner)` and the gating scorers check the returned verdict shape.' This reuses the already-tested offline path in `assemble_diff_context` without requiring fixture git history.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for routing conflict-marker detection through DET/Engine-B rather than a scripted `workflow_ops` step that greps `steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context`; if the scripted approach is simpler and equally correct, consider updating AC8 to reflect it.
findings.finding: AC8 routes the conflict-marker check through the DET/Engine-B infrastructure ('wired as an advisory fail-open DET criterion'), but conflict markers are a deterministic grep on diff text that is already assembled in memory by the `assemble_diff` step. The DET path requires a detector plugin, Engine-B's scan machinery, and tool availability — all unnecessary for a pattern that's a single `'<<<<<<<' in diff_text` check. A scripted step in `workflow_ops.py` reading `steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context` would detect conflict markers more simply, with no tool-availability failure mode, no plugin registration, and no Engine-B overhead. The plan provides no positive rationale for choosing DET over a direct scripted check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'conflict-marker deterministic detector wired as an advisory fail-open DET criterion'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'content-triggered' overlay mechanism: specify the predicate (e.g., 'net deleted lines > threshold', 'no test files in changed_files'), where it's computed (new scripted step? new field in criteria_routing.json?), and how it differs from the existing glob/recommend trigger modes — so implementers don't design this seam independently.
findings.finding: AC4 introduces a 'content-triggered overlay capability' for 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' overlays, but the plan doesn't define what 'content-triggered' means mechanistically. The existing `overlay_union` supports two trigger modes: glob-triggered (applies_to file-path globs) and recommend-triggered (base reviewer escalation). 'Content-triggered' implies a new modality — e.g., triggered by diff properties such as net line deletions, absence of test changes, or commit message content — but the plan doesn't state: (a) what predicate defines a content trigger, (b) where it lives (new field in criteria_routing.json? new scripted step? new logic in overlay_union?), or (c) how 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' differ from each other in triggering logic. Implementers must make this design choice without guidance.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'content-triggered overlay' mechanically in the plan: is it (a) a new deterministic trigger type on diff content distinct from glob-match and LLM-escalation, or (b) a labeling convention for overlays that are only LLM-escalated? If (a), justify the novelty vs recommend_overlays and document the new trigger contract.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a 'content-triggered overlay capability' as a new AC item, but never defines whether this is a mechanically distinct third trigger type (e.g., regex/AST match on diff content) beyond the two already-shipped mechanisms (file-glob Round-A and LLM-escalation Round-B via recommend_overlays). If it IS a third mechanism, its novelty vs the existing recommend_overlays path — which already fires overlays based on diff content via the base reviewer — is not justified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the code-review eval case payload shape (e.g. `diff_text` + `changed_files` keys, or a two-commit fixture) and add the corresponding `eval_solver.run_case` branch so the code-review arm is concretely described and scaffolding is unambiguous before other ACs depend on it.
findings.finding: The plan's foundational AC ('eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers exists and gates the rest') asserts a new eval_solver capability, but the integration path has a concrete scaffolding gap: `case_store` initialises a repo with a single commit (no HEAD~1), so a code-review eval case cannot be run against a git range — and the plan does not specify how eval cases supply a reviewable diff (inline `diff_text` in the case dict, a second fixture commit, or another mechanism).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet: 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `code-review` arm to `eval_solver.run_case` that reads `diff_text` and `changed_files` from the case dict and calls `shim.review_code(diff_text=..., changed_files=..., runner=runner, repo_root=root)`; document the case YAML keys (`diff_text`, `changed_files`, `expect`) in `eval_specs/README.plan-review.md` (or a new `README.code-review.md`). No change to `case_store` is required if `diff_text` is supplied inline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'both halves' of the behavioral-testing standard explicitly in the AC (e.g., 'half 1: test existence for new/changed behavior; half 2: assertion-quality / edge-case coverage') so the overlay enrichment is implementable and the AC is checkable.
findings.finding: The AC 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)' references 'both halves' but never defines what the two halves are. The existing code-review-tests.md (tool-verified) has no 'halves' concept — it is a generic test-sufficiency instruction. An executor cannot determine what 'full behavioral-testing standard' means or what constitutes completion of this AC.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves); Pass 2 stays generic'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'both halves' with explicit labels, e.g., 'tests overlay checks (a) behavior existence — new/changed behavior has at least one test, and (b) assertion quality — tests assert meaningful postconditions, not just that the call succeeds.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define each named Pass-2 verifier upgrade dimension (contradiction, caller, arithmetic, absence) with a one-sentence description and specify what 'prefer AST-LSP' means as a concrete prompt instruction.
findings.finding: The AC 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP)' names four verification dimensions and a tool-preference protocol, but none of these are defined anywhere in the plan or in the existing code-review-verify.md (tool-verified). An executor must invent what 'caller' means in a code-review context, what 'prefer AST-LSP' translates to as a prompt instruction, and how these four dimensions map to the existing binary sub-answer schema.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'content-triggered' overlay mechanism: specify what differentiates it from a glob trigger, what field/schema carries the content predicate in criteria_routing.json, and how the workflow step evaluates it — then map this spec to the 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' overlay entries.
findings.finding: The AC 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it' references two new overlay IDs ('deletion-impact', 'scope-intent') that are not in the current OVERLAY_IDS tuple (tool-verified: 11 entries, neither present), and the plan never defines what 'content-triggered' means mechanically — no trigger schema, no applies_to equivalent, no routing entry shape for content vs. glob triggers.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split this AC into separate ACs, each with a defined schema or format: (a) FP-ledger: file location + field schema; (b) grounding-effort telemetry: field name + type + emission point; (c) approach-viability note: schema + placement in review output; (d) confirm whether 'coach moves' means enriching MOVE_REGISTRY or a new artifact.
findings.finding: The AC 'FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note landed' references four distinct artifacts but defines none of their schemas, file locations, or formats. 'FP-ledger convention' has no defined structure. 'Grounding-effort/health telemetry' has no defined field names or emission points. 'Approach-viability note' has no defined schema or placement in the review output.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note landed'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Each non-f93a child that gates on an eval explicitly lists f93a as a prerequisite (blocked-by or depends-on) in its story metadata.
findings.finding: Children da8a, 4b56, da25, f983, 3c5d, 7c58, and 1669 all carry ACs requiring 'eval passes' or similar, but none formally declares a dependency on f93a delivering the eval harness first. If f93a is merged out of order or delayed, those children's gating ACs become unverifiable at merge time.
findings.location: All children except f93a-b947-558b-469c
findings.suggested_fix: Add to each non-f93a child's metadata: 'Depends on: f93a (eval foundation).' At minimum add a note in the Scope or Acceptance Criteria section: 'Requires f93a eval harness to be merged first.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Child 3c5d explicitly declares a dependency on f983's content-trigger seam and confirms the seam supports trailer-presence as a trigger predicate type, not just diff-content regex.
findings.finding: Child 3c5d uses the content-triggered overlay seam (registry.py content-trigger) that child f983 creates, but 3c5d does not formally declare f983 as a prerequisite. If 3c5d is worked or merged before f983, the content-trigger mechanism it depends on does not exist.
findings.location: child 3c5d-b21a-9af2-470a
findings.suggested_fix: In 3c5d's Scope or AC, add: 'Depends on f983 content-trigger seam; confirm seam supports trailer-presence predicate before implementing.' Alternatively, if 3c5d's trigger is distinct enough (trailer-parse rather than diff-content regex), document it as a separate trigger type and own the registry.py extension in 3c5d's scope explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly assign ownership of verify-drop signal emission to a child (likely 1669 or 4b56): instrument the Pass-2 execution path to count and emit dropped findings before 1669's metric consumer reads them.
findings.finding: Child 1669's verify-drop rate health metric requires Pass-2 to emit a signal when it drops a finding, but child 4b56 (which enriches the Pass-2 verifier prompt) does not mention instrumenting or emitting any drop-rate telemetry signal. The data source for this metric is unowned.
findings.location: child 1669-a499-e540-46a4 (metric consumer) and child 4b56-4682-bb34-4fd1 (Pass-2 owner)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 1669's scope (or 4b56's scope if it touches the Pass-2 runner): 'Instrument Pass-2 execution path to emit a drop-count signal (number of findings not passed through after verification) so the verify-drop rate metric in coverage.metrics has a real data source.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
